summary_en: Cost of capital remeasured at 6% early-2020s band using multi-method long-term time-series analysis. Management target: consolidated operating profit >¥10B + ROE >5% maintained and further improved. FY2025 results: operating profit ¥14.9B, ROE 7.8%, dividend per share ¥86 (raised), share buyback ¥3.4B (¥600M increase), total shareholder return ratio 98.5%. PBR >1.0x and TSR materially above TOPIX in stable conditions through Apr 2025.
title_en: Update on Measures for Realizing Management Conscious of Cost of Capital and Share Price, and Status of Shareholder Dialogue – as of May 2026
translation_en_brief:
On May 14, 2026, Japan Securities Finance filed an update to the Tokyo Stock Exchange on measures to realize management conscious of cost of capital and share price. The company remeasured its cost of capital using objective long-term time-series data across multiple methodologies, arriving at an estimate in the early-6% band. The company states this reflects its natural capital structure given lower relative financial and business-strategy risk inherent in licensed securities-finance operations with regulatory capital requirements and constrained business scope.

Management targets remain: consolidated operating profit consistently above ¥10 billion and ROE exceeding 5%, with further improvement targeted. FY2025 results exceeded these targets with operating profit of ¥14.9 billion and ROE of 7.8%. The company increased dividend per share to ¥86 and executed share buybacks of ¥3.4 billion (including a ¥600 million increase from initial plan), achieving a total shareholder return ratio of 98.5%. The company notes that its share price (PBR >1.0x) and total shareholder return (materially above TOPIX) remained stable except during April 2025 when U.S. tariff-policy uncertainty created temporary weakness, demonstrating market appreciation of operating performance.
